title: Assign or correct a Spend Category
preconditions: The Canonical Transaction and chosen active Spend Category exist; any automatic suggestion has been produced by CLASSIFY, not LEDGER.
postconditions: The transaction has zero or one explicit active Category Assignment; correction provenance is inspectable; hierarchy roll-ups are deterministic; original bank facts and independent dimensions are unchanged.
main_flow: 1. The caller retrieves the active transaction and hierarchical category catalogue. 2. The owner selects one active category node. 3. Tally validates transaction lifecycle, category lifecycle, and one-active-assignment cardinality. 4. Tally appends an assign or correct event with actor and reason. 5. Direct category membership and current ancestor roll-ups expose the transaction exactly once without changing transaction facts or any other financial dimension.
alt_flows: 1a. Transaction does not exist -> return not found
2a. Category is absent or archived -> reject without changing the active assignment
3a. A second simultaneous category or split allocation is requested -> reject with a stable cardinality error
4a. The requested assignment matches the active decision -> return an idempotent no-change success
metadata: {"depth_tier":2,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner wants an uncategorized transaction classified or an existing category decision corrected.","status":"draft"}
